---
name: api-project-structure
description: Reference for the Go API project structure used with newapi and go-bootstrap. Use this when asked to understand, navigate, or modify the generated code structure of a Go API project using gin and go-bootstrap.
---

## newapi Project Code Structure

Projects generated by `newapi` follow a three-layer architecture: **endpoint → service → schema**, all within the `internal/` package.

### Directory Layout

```
<project>/
├── go.mod
├── cmd/
│   └── server/
│       └── main.go              # server entry point (imports _ internal/endpoints)
└── internal/
    ├── endpoints/               # HTTP layer: route registration, method binding
    │   └── {name}_ep.go
    ├── services/                # Business logic layer: Swagger annotations, core logic
    │   └── {name}_srv.go
    └── schemas/                 # Data layer: request/response struct definitions
        └── {name}_schema.go
```

File naming: PascalCase API name (e.g. `GetUser`) → snake_case filename (e.g. `get_user`).

---

### Endpoint Layer (`internal/endpoints/{name}_ep.go`)

**Responsibility**: Register routes and bind HTTP methods; dispatch requests to the Service layer.

```go
// Code generated by newapi. DO NOT EDIT.
type GetUserEndpoint struct {
    ginBoot.BaseEndpoint
    ginBoot.JsonCodecEp
    ginBoot.GETEndpoint      // matches HTTP method
}

func (e *GetUserEndpoint) Path() string {
    return "/user/:id"       // {id} path param converted to :id
}

func (e *GetUserEndpoint) HandlerFunc() gin.HandlerFunc {
    return ginBoot.Endpoint(e, services.GetUserService)
}

func init() {
    var endpoint GetUserEndpoint
    endpoint.SetMiddleware(services.GetUserSetMiddleware())
    ginBoot.Server().AddEndpoint(&endpoint)
}
```

**Guidance**: This file rarely needs manual edits. To add middleware, update `SetMiddleware` in the service layer.

---

### Service Layer (`internal/services/{name}_srv.go`)

**Responsibility**: Business logic and full Swagger documentation annotations.

```go
func GetUserSetMiddleware() []gin.HandlerFunc {
    return []gin.HandlerFunc{}  // add auth, rate-limit middleware here
}

// GetUserService godoc
//
//  @Summary     GetUser
//  @Description Get user info
//  @Tags        user
//  @Router      /user/{id} [GET]
//  @Accept      json
//  @Produce     json
//  @Param       req   body     schemas.GetUserServiceReq  true "request body"
//  @Param       id    path     string                     true "id"
//  @Success     200   {object} schemas.GetUserServiceResp
func GetUserService(c context.Context, req *schemas.GetUserServiceReq) (resp *schemas.GetUserServiceResp, err error) {
    resp = new(schemas.GetUserServiceResp)
    // implement business logic here
    return
}
```

**Guidance**:
- Files whose first line does **not** contain `Code generated by newapi. DO NOT EDIT.` will never be overwritten.
- Fill in the function body; `resp` is already initialized.
- Return middleware slices from `SetMiddleware`.

---

### Schema Layer (`internal/schemas/{name}_schema.go`)

**Responsibility**: Define request and response data structures.

```go
// swagger:response GetUserServiceResp
type GetUserServiceResp struct {
    ID   int64  `json:"id"`
    Name string `json:"name"`
}

// swagger:parameters GetUserServiceReq
type GetUserServiceReq struct {
    ID int64 `json:"id" form:"id"`
}
```

**Guidance**:
- Schema files are **never overwritten** — edit freely.
- Add JSON tags, validation tags, etc. here.

---

### Code Generation Marker

| Condition | Behavior on re-run |
|-----------|-------------------|
| File first line contains `Code generated by newapi. DO NOT EDIT.` | File **will be overwritten** |
| File first line does not contain the marker | File **will be skipped** |
| Schema file (any content) | **Never overwritten** |

Remove the marker line from an endpoint or service file to protect it from being overwritten.
